steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that Euro-American farmers used Haudenosaunee techniques "without" having seen them firsthand, and it rules out independent development. This creates a causality signal — how could the techniques spread without direct observation or duplication?

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice A explains the spread via intermediaries — people "more directly influenced" than the farmers.

This directly addresses the causality gap implied by the signal.

The other choices ignore or reverse the logic:
  • B shifts focus to crops (not techniques)
  • C misplaces the timeline/region
  • D contradicts the "early nineteenth century" timeframe
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
